    78	 succeeded in 0ms:
    79	---
    80	name: codex-review
    81	version: 2.1.4
    82	author: BenedictKing
    83	description: "Professional code review skill for Claude Code. Automatically collects file changes and task status. Triggers when working directory has uncommitted changes, or reviews latest commit when clean. Triggers: code review, review, 代码审核, 代码审查, 检查代码"
    84	allowed-tools:
    85	  - Task
    86	  - Bash
    87	  - Read
    88	  - Glob
    89	  - Write
    90	  - Edit
    91	user-invocable: true
    92	---
    93	
    94	# Codex Code Review Skill
    95	
    96	## Trigger Conditions
    97	
    98	Triggered when user input contains:
    99	
   100	- "代码审核", "代码审查", "审查代码", "审核代码"
   101	- "review", "code review", "review code", "codex 审核"
   102	- "帮我审核", "检查代码", "审一下", "看看代码"
   103	
   104	## Core Concept: Intention vs Implementation
   105	
   106	Running `codex review --uncommitted` alone only shows AI "what was done (Implementation)".
   107	Recording intention first tells AI "what you wanted to do (Intention)".
   108	
   109	**"Code changes + intention description" as combined input is the most effective way to improve AI code review quality.**
   110	
   111	## Skill Architecture
   112	
   113	This skill operates in two phases:
   114	
   115	1. **Preparation Phase** (current context): Check working directory, update CHANGELOG
   116	2. **Review Phase** (isolated context): Invoke Task tool to execute Lint + codex review (using context: fork to reduce context waste)
   117	
   118	## Execution Steps
   119	
   120	### 0. [First] Check Working Directory Status
   121	
   122	```bash
   123	git diff --name-only && git status --short
   124	```
   125	
   126	**Decide review mode based on output:**
   127	
   128	- **Has uncommitted changes** → Continue with steps 1-4 (normal flow)
   129	- **Clean working directory** → Directly invoke codex-runner: `codex review --commit HEAD`
   130	
   131	### 1. [Mandatory] Check if CHANGELOG is Updated
   132	
   133	**Before any review, must check if CHANGELOG.md contains description of current changes.**
   134	
   135	```bash
   136	# Check if CHANGELOG.md is in uncommitted changes
   137	git diff --name-only | grep -E "(CHANGELOG|changelog)"
   138	```
   139	
   140	**If CHANGELOG is not updated, you must automatically perform the following (don't ask user to do it manually):**
   141	
   142	1. **Analyze changes**: Run `git diff --stat` and `git diff` to get complete changes
   143	2. **Auto-generate CHANGELOG entry**: Generate compliant entry based on code changes
   144	3. **Write to CHANGELOG.md**: Use Edit tool to insert entry at top of `[Unreleased]` section
   145	4. **Continue review flow**: Immediately proceed to next steps after CHANGELOG update
   146	
   147	**Auto-generated CHANGELOG entry format:**
   148	
   149	```markdown
   150	## [Unreleased]
   151	
   152	### Added / Changed / Fixed
   153	
   154	- Feature description: what problem was solved or what functionality was implemented
   155	- Affected files: main modified files/modules
   156	```
   157	
   158	**Example - Auto-generation Flow:**
   159	
   160	```
   161	1. Detected CHANGELOG not updated
   162	2. Run git diff --stat, found handlers/responses.go modified (+88 lines)
   163	3. Run git diff to analyze details: added CompactHandler function
   164	4. Auto-generate entry:
   165	   ### Added
   166	   - Added `/v1/responses/compact` endpoint for conversation context compression
   167	   - Supports multi-channel failover and request body size limits
   168	5. Use Edit tool to write to CHANGELOG.md
   169	6. Continue with lint and codex review
   170	```
   171	
   172	### 2. [Critical] Stage All New Files
   173	
   174	**Before invoking codex review, must add all new files (untracked files) to git staging area, otherwise codex will report P1 error.**
   175	
   176	```bash
   177	# Check for new files
   178	git status --short | grep "^??"
   179	```
   180	
   181	**If there are new files, automatically execute:**
   182	
   183	```bash
   184	# Safely stage all new files (handles empty list and special filenames)
   185	git ls-files --others --exclude-standard -z | while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do git add -- "$f"; done
   186	```
   187	
   188	**Explanation:**
   189	
   190	- `-z` uses null character to separate filenames, correctly handles filenames with spaces/newlines
   191	- `while IFS= read -r -d ''` reads filenames one by one
   192	- `git add -- "$f"` uses `--` separator, correctly handles filenames starting with `-`
   193	- When no new files exist, loop body doesn't execute, safely skipped
   194	- This won't stage modified files, only handles new files
   195	- codex needs files to be tracked by git for proper review
   196	
   197	### 3. Evaluate Task Difficulty and Invoke codex-runner
   198	
   199	**Count change scale:**
   200	
   201	```bash
   202	# Count number of changed files and lines of code
   203	git diff --stat | tail -1
   204	```
   205	
   206	**Difficulty Assessment Criteria:**
   207	
   208	**Difficult Tasks** (meets any condition):
   209	
   210	- Modified files ≥ 10
   211	- Total code changes (insertions + deletions) ≥ 500 lines
   212	- Single metric: insertions ≥ 300 lines OR deletions ≥ 300 lines
   213	- Involves core architecture/algorithm changes
   214	- Cross-module refactoring
   215	- Config: `model_reasoning_effort=xhigh`, timeout 30 minutes
   216	
   217	**Normal Tasks** (other cases):
   218	
   219	- Config: `model_reasoning_effort=high`, timeout 10 minutes
   220

codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r2-20260523.md:7843:codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523-prefix-evidence.md:6628:./codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-http-3.16.0/package/CHANGELOG.md:295:  - This release introduces an `invalidateExisting` flag to the `CreateReadWriteSession` and `Oauth` activities. If enabled, this will invalidate existing read-write and oauth API keys. This is useful in scenarios where a user attempts to create numerous `ReadWrite` or `Oauth` sessions. Because our API caps the number of session keys associated with a user, this flag can clear all other existing session keys of that specific type (e.g. setting `invalidateExisting: true` for `CreateReadWriteSession` will invalidate all previously created read-write session keys)

codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:11819:    44	`@turnkey/http` provides fully typed http fetchers for interacting with the Turnkey API. codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:11998:| `src/Http.cs:226`, `:247`, `:280`, `:304` | `public_api.types.ts:2171`, `:2193`, `:2715`, `:2803` | C# omits optional `generateAppProofs`; upstream request DTOs can include it. | Add `bool? codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:11999:| `src/Http.cs:121`, `:136`, `:147`, `:158`, `:169` | `...public_api.client.ts:1630`, `:2900`, `:2963`, `:2995`, `:3089` | C# throws for empty whoami org and null bodies; upstream stamp methods do no such validation before `JSON.stringify`. | Remove for strict parity, or document as intentional validation and test as non-parity. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:12003:Not satisfied. `tests/Fixtures/` currently has `encoding`, `crypto`, and `api-key-stamper` fixtures only; there is no `tests/Fixtures/http` fixture exercising `src/Http.cs`. `tests/HttpTests.cs` uses inline assertions and verifies shape/header decodability, but it does not assert byte-for-byte parity against Node output from pinned `@turnkey/http@3.16.0`.
codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:12008:- Exact Node byte parity for `Http.cs` could not be verified because no HTTP fixtures/generator outputs are present.

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-http-3.16.0/package/README.md | sed -n '1,48p'" in /Users/takeshi/Kyuzan/src/turnkey-sdk-csharp
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# @turnkey/http
     2	
     3	[![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/@turnkey/http?color=%234C48FF)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@turnkey/http)
     4	
     5	A lower-level, fully typed HTTP client for interacting with [Turnkey](https://turnkey.com) API.
     6	
     7	For signing transactions and messages, check out the higher-level [`@turnkey/ethers`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@turnkey/ethers) or [`@turnkey/viem`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@turnkey/viem) signers.
     8	
     9	Turnkey API documentation lives here: https://docs.turnkey.com.
    10	
    11	## Getting started
    12	
    13	```bash
    14	$ npm install @turnkey/http
    15	```
    16	
    17	```typescript
    18	import { ApiKeyStamper } from "@turnkey/api-key-stamper";
    19	import { TurnkeyClient } from "@turnkey/http";
    20	
    21	// This stamper produces signatures using the API key pair passed in.
    22	const stamper = new ApiKeyStamper({
    23	  apiPublicKey: "...",
    24	  apiPrivateKey: "...",
    25	});
    26	
    27	// The Turnkey client uses the passed in stamper to produce signed requests
    28	// and sends them to Turnkey
    29	const client = new TurnkeyClient(
    30	  {
    31	    baseUrl: "https://api.turnkey.com",
    32	  },
    33	  stamper,
    34	);
    35	
    36	// Now you can make authenticated requests!
    37	const data = await client.getWhoami({
    38	  organizationId: "<Your organization id>",
    39	});
    40	```
    41	
    42	## HTTP fetchers
    43	
    44	`@turnkey/http` provides fully typed http fetchers for interacting with the Turnkey API. You can find all available methods [here](/packages/http/src/__generated__/services/coordinator/public/v1/public_api.fetcher.ts). The types of input parameters and output responses are also exported for convenience.
    45	
    46	The OpenAPI spec that generates all fetchers is also [included](/packages/http/src/__generated__/services/coordinator/public/v1/public_api.swagger.json) in the package.
    47	
    48	## `withAsyncPolling(...)` helper

A. **Source Pin Acknowledgement**

- Upstream package: `@turnkey/http`
- Upstream version: `3.16.0`
- Tarball sha256: `d849f2156633f63062c52067785df1ce33eac0659044df932862c5d6de9dbdaf`
- C# file git SHA: `85e769e83f6befaf338b1159dd7bb1909eca5025`

B. **Method Coverage Table**

| C# method (file:line) | Upstream TS function (path:line) | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---:|---|
| `Http(...)` `src/Http.cs:62` | `TurnkeyClient.constructor` `ts-source/__generated__/services/coordinator/public/v1/public_api.client.ts:435` | REVIEWED | Stores config/stamper equivalent; baseUrl validation divergence in E. |
| `GetHttpClient` `src/Http.cs:82` | README construction pattern `package/README.md:18`, `TurnkeyClient.constructor` `public_api.client.ts:435` | REVIEWED | C# credential-bundle factory has no direct `@turnkey/http` function; request-client construction reviewed, crypto decrypt path is outside this file. |
| `FromTargetPrivateKey` `src/Http.cs:114` | README construction pattern `package/README.md:18`, `TurnkeyClient.constructor` `public_api.client.ts:435` | REVIEWED | C# derives the public key before creating `ApiKeyStamper`; upstream expects caller-provided stamper. |
| `StampGetWhoami` `src/Http.cs:143` | `stampGetWhoami` `public_api.client.ts:1630` | REVIEWED | Endpoint/body/stamp flow reviewed; pre-validation divergence in E. |
| `StampInitImportPrivateKey` `src/Http.cs:158` | `stampInitImportPrivateKey` `public_api.client.ts:3089` | REVIEWED | Endpoint/body/stamp flow reviewed; DTO order/default divergences in E. |
| `StampImportPrivateKey` `src/Http.cs:169` | `stampImportPrivateKey` `public_api.client.ts:2995` | REVIEWED | Endpoint/body/stamp flow reviewed; DTO order/default divergences in E. |
| `StampExportPrivateKey` `src/Http.cs:180` | `stampExportPrivateKey` `public_api.client.ts:2900` | REVIEWED | Endpoint/body/stamp flow reviewed; DTO order/default divergences in E. |
| `StampExportWalletAccount` `src/Http.cs:191` | `stampExportWalletAccount` `public_api.client.ts:2963` | REVIEWED | Endpoint/body/stamp flow reviewed; DTO order/default divergences in E. |
| `CreateSignedRequest` `src/Http.cs:199` | repeated generated stamp pattern, e.g. `public_api.client.ts:1631`; `TSignedRequest` `base.ts:270` | REVIEWED | C# helper factors repeated TS generated code; return DTO order divergence in E. |

C. **Intentional Adaptations**

- C# nested DTO classes replace TS type aliases/interfaces: structural language adaptation for typed serialization.
- `CreateSignedRequest` factors repeated generated TS stamp methods into one helper: same intended sequence of body string, stamp, and URL assembly.
- `System.Text.Json` source-generation replaces `JSON.stringify`: structural IL2CPP/AOT adaptation, provided DTO order/null behavior is kept byte-compatible.
- Concrete `ApiKeyStamper` dependency replaces upstream `TStamper` interface: structural narrowing to this port’s API-key signing subset.

D. **D17 Enforcement Check**

N/A (not Crypto.cs).

E. **Logic Divergence Findings**

| C# file:line | TS upstream path:line | Semantic diff | Suggested fix |
|---|---|---|---|
| `src/Http.cs:62` | `public_api.client.ts:435` | TS rejects missing/falsy `baseUrl`; C# accepts `null`/empty `baseUrl`, producing relative URLs like `/public/v1/...`. | Add `string.IsNullOrEmpty(baseUrl)` validation in the constructor/factories. |
| `src/Http.cs:143`, `:158`, `:169`, `:180`, `:191` | `public_api.client.ts:1630`, `:3089`, `:2995`, `:2900`, `:2963` | C# throws for empty whoami org and null body inputs; upstream stamp methods perform no runtime validation before `JSON.stringify`. | For strict parity, remove these preconditions; otherwise document them as intentional non-parity. |
| `src/Http.cs:220` | `base.ts:270`, `public_api.client.ts:1634` | C# `SignedRequest` property order is `url, body, stamp`; upstream `TSignedRequest` and returned objects are `body, stamp, url`. Observable if callers serialize the returned bundle. | Reorder `SignedRequest` properties and object initializer to `Body`, `Stamp`, `Url`. |
| `src/Http.cs:250`, `:279`, `:316`, `:344` | `public_api.types.ts:2163`, `:2185`, `:2707`, `:2795` | Submit request DTO order is `organizationId,type,timestampMs,parameters`; upstream generated request declarations are `type,timestampMs,organizationId,parameters,generateAppProofs`. | Reorder declarations or use `[JsonPropertyOrder]`, then lock with Node fixtures. |
| `src/Http.cs:298` | `public_api.types.ts:2695` | `ImportPrivateKeyParameters` order is `userId,addressFormats,curve,encryptedBundle,privateKeyName`; upstream intent order is `userId,privateKeyName,encryptedBundle,curve,addressFormats`. | Reorder DTO fields or set explicit JSON property order. |
| `src/Http.cs:244` onward | TS runtime `JSON.stringify(input)` in `public_api.client.ts:1632`, `:2905`, `:2968`, `:3000`, `:3094` | C# DTO default initializers serialize unset required fields as `""`, `{}`, or `[]`; upstream objects with omitted properties omit those keys. | Use required init properties/no default wire values, or add explicit validation before serialization. |

F. **Fixture Comparison Gate**

Not satisfied. `tests/Fixtures/` contains `encoding`, `crypto`, and `api-key-stamper` fixtures, but no `tests/Fixtures/http` fixture exercising `src/Http.cs`. `tests/Fixtures/README.md:26` advertises an `http/` fixture directory, but it is absent.

`tests/HttpTests.cs` uses inline assertions for URLs/body shape and verifies that the stamp header decodes (`tests/HttpTests.cs:46`, `:66`, `:100`, `:132`, `:157`, `:181`), but it does not assert byte-for-byte parity against Node output generated from pinned `@turnkey/http@3.16.0`.

G. **Unresolved Assumptions**

- Exact Node byte parity for `Http.cs` remains unverified because there is no HTTP fixture/generator output.
- I did not run `dotnet test`; this review was source/fixture inspection under the current read-only sandbox.
- `GetHttpClient` credential-bundle decryption parity depends on `Crypto.cs`, not `@turnkey/http`, so only the HTTP construction/stamping surface was reviewed here.

Round 2 pass criterion: **not met** because E has divergence entries and F lacks matching HTTP fixtures.
